as_of.title: Global Economic Developments and the U.S. Economy

inflation.label: oil + tariffs
inflation.note: Disinflation stalled on tariffs and inflation then moved notably higher on energy costs; expects both to wane but risks tilted up.
inflation.quote: Disinflation in the U.S. stalled over the preceding year, largely because of increased tariffs. In recent months, inflation moved notably higher because of higher energy costs.

labor.label: downside risks
labor.note: Labor market broadly stable with low hiring and firing, but risks skewed to the downside.
labor.quote: The U.S. labor market is broadly stable, with both hiring and firing at relatively low levels. I see risks to the labor market as somewhat skewed to the downside.

stance.label: appropriate / well positioned
stance.note: Backs holding the funds rate at 3-1/2 to 3-3/4 percent, viewing the stance as well positioned and data-dependent.
stance.quote: I believe this policy stance leaves us well positioned to respond to economic developments based on the incoming data, the evolving outlook, and the balance of risks.
